translations.translation:
Wen Hui (Manji)
溫恢 (曼基)
Lived ADca.181–ca.225
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]

byZL181
onJanuary 12th, 2024

Wen Hui, styledManji, was fromQi[祁],Taiyuan[太原].
When his father, [Wen] Shu, who was theGrand AdministratorofZhuo commandery[涿郡太守], died,[Wen] Huiwas fifteen. He took part in the funeral procession that returned to his homevillage.1•••
Wen Huiescorted the corpse of his father back to their hometown for it to be buried.↩
His household had sufficient wealth.[Wen] Huisaid, “The world is about to be embroiled in chaos. What do I need wealth for?” In one day, he had distributed it all, generously giving it to his clansmen. His hometown thought of him highly and compared him to Xun Yue [郇越].2•••
Xun Yue, styled Chenzhong, was a man who lived during the late WesternHan dynasty. He gave millions of his wealth that he inherited from his ancestors to his clansmen, up to the ninth degree.↩
He was nominated asFilial and Incorrupt. He served asChiefofLinqiu[廩丘長],PrefectofYanling[鄢陵令],PrefectofGuangchuan[廣川令],ChancellorofPengcheng[彭城相], andChancellorofLu[魯相]. Wherever he was, he was praised. He entered to serve asMaster of Recordsunder theAssisting Chancellor, then left to beInspectorofYang province[揚州刺史]. TheGrand Progenitorsaid, “I very much want to place you close and near me,3but I know that is incomparable to how important the affairs of that province are. This is why theBook of Documentsstated, ‘How virtuous thighs and upper arms are!4How orderly a myriad of affairs are!’ And how can it not be when you haveJiang Jiserving asOfficial of the Government Seatunder you?” At the time,[Jiang] Jiwas madeGrand AdministratorofDanyang[丹楊太守], thus[Jiang] Jiwas sent back to his province.5He told toZhang LiaoandYue Jin, among others, saying, “TheInspectorofYang province[揚州刺史] has proficiency in his understanding of military matters. Regardless of action or inaction, consult them together withhim.”•••
All of the locations in this paragraph mentioned beforeYang provincewere relatively near whereCao Caooften was.↩
Here, “thighs and upper arms” [股肱] is a metaphor for highly trusted and capable ministers.↩
Jiang Jiwas fromYang province, hence the usage of “back to his province”.↩
In the twenty-fourth year of theera [219],Sun QuanattackedHefei[合肥]. At the time, the provinces all defended themselves.[Wen] Huitold theInspectorofYan province[兖州刺史],Pei Qian, “Although the area hasenemies, they are not worth worrying over. Instead, I fear that theGeneral who Conquers the Southis suffering from vicissitudes. Now the water is rising whilstZixiaoand his excursion army6have no defenses that far out.Guan Yuis energetic and powerful, and should he seize the advantage and advance forth, there will certainly be disaster.” Soon, there was the incident ofFancheng[樊城].7An edict summoned[Pei] Qianand theInspectorofYu province[豫州刺史],Lü Gong, among others, but[Pei] Qianand the others tarried.[Wen] Huidiscreetly told[Pei] Qian, “It must be the urgent situation atXiangyang[襄陽] that they want us to go there. The reason why there is no urgent assembly is because they do not want to scare the troops that are afar. In one or two days, there will be a secret message urging you, sir, to advance onto the roads.Zhang Liaoand many others will also be summoned.[Zhang] Liaoand the others have always known the intentions of theKing, and should they come here first after receiving their summons, you, sir, shall receive their condemnation!”[Pei] Qianheeded his words, set up his military supplies, then soon quickly dispatched [soldiers] with light supplies. He would indeed receive an urgent order, and[Zhang] Liaoand the others each received their summons soon after. This was according to[Wen] Huiexpected.•••
“Excursion armies” [縣軍] tend to be small and go deep into enemy territory, often without reinforcements.↩
This refers toGuan Yudestroying the forces ofFeichengxian.↩
After theCivil Emperorascended the throne steps,[Wen] Huiserved asPalace Attendant, then left to serve asGrand AdministratorofWei commandery[魏郡太守]. After several years, he was promoted toInspectorofLiang province[涼州刺史],bearing tallyin leading asColonel who Protects the Qiang. He fell ill on the road and died. He was forty-five at the time. An edict stated:
”[Wen] Huihas the characteristics of pillars and foundation stones. He was in service to thedeparted emperor, where his deeds and diligence were clear and evident. Because he would undertake matters for me and was loyal to the imperial family, I therefore gave him a responsibility tens of thousands ofliaway and entrusted him with the affairs of that particular area. Why must it be that he can no longer realize them? I feel extreme pity!”
The son of[Wen] Hui,[Wen] Sheng, was conferred peerage ofMarquis within the Passes.[Wen] Shengdied early, thus the peerage terminated. After[Wen] Huidied,Meng JianofRunan[汝南] served asInspectorofLiang province[涼州刺史], possessing achievements in governance. His office reachedGeneral who Conquers the East.A
TheSummary of Weistates:[Meng] Jianwas styledGongwei. When he was young, he andZhuge Liangtraveled together to study. After[Zhuge] Liangmarched toQi mountain[祁山], when he replied to the message of theCommanding Kingof the Sima clan, he instructedDuZixuto send his regards toGongwei.
